Name: Operation Providing Hope
Description:
Founded in North Carolina in the aftermath of Hurricane Helene, which left the state’s Mountain region devastated, Operation Providing Hope is a veteran-led, volunteer-driven nonprofit organization dedicated to delivering search and rescue, medical care, and disaster relief when communities need it most.

Led by military veterans and medical professionals, our organization brings discipline, operational leadership, and real-world emergency response expertise to the frontlines of disaster. From hurricanes and floods to wildfires and complex emergency situations, we are committed to rapid mobilization and decisive action in some of the most challenging and hazardous environments.

Our team includes firefighters, paramedics, medical professionals, and disaster response specialists who step forward in moments of crisis. Whether pulling individuals to safety, providing critical medical treatment, delivering supplies, or supporting families in the immediate aftermath of disaster, our members serve with courage, professionalism, and compassion.

At Operation Providing Hope, our mission is simple but vital: save lives, support recovery, and restore hope. Through service, teamwork, and unwavering commitment, we stand ready to answer the call—ensuring that no community faces disaster alone.

Name: Protezione Civile
Description:
Ho creato questa pagina per coloro che lavorano o svolgono dei corsi di ripetizione presso il Consorzio Protezione Civile Regione Lugano Città.

Perché abbiamo bisogno della protezione civile?

Protezione e sicurezza per la popolazione

Il nostro Paese è densamente popolato. Le nostre infrastrutture sono altamente tecnologiche. La nostra società è fortemente interconnessa e quindi sempre più vulnerabile. E' pertanto necessario proteggere la popolazione e le sue basi vitali.
 
Ampio ventaglio di compiti

Per proteggerci dagli effetti di gravi eventi, catastrofi, situazioni d'emergenza e conflitti armati, la protezione civile svolge i seguenti compiti:

Protegge, soccorre, vettovaglia ed assiste le persone colpite.

Supporta e rinforza gli organi di condotta e le organizzazioni partner, in particolare i mezzi di pronto intervento della polizia, dei pompieri e della sanità.

Protegge beni culturali preziosi ed esegue lavori di ripristino.
 
Anche a vantaggio dell'economia

Anche le aziende beneficiano delle prestazioni della protezione civile. Le misure di protezione limitano i danni causati alle aziende dalle catastrofi. Vengono inoltre sostenute con misure transitorie e di ripristino affinché possano riprendere rapidamente la produzione.
 
Accumulare esperienze 

Il sistema di milizia permette ai militi di accumulare esperienze utili anche per la loro vita privata e professionale. Sapersi integrare rapidamente in una squadra, formarsi e specializzarsi in un nuovo campo ed impegnarsi per la società e la comunità: tutto ciò permette di allargare i propri orizzonti. Ne traggono vantaggio soprattutto i quadri che vengono addestrati ad assumere una funzione di comando anche in situazioni difficili.

Name: UK International Search & Rescue
Description:
The UK ISAR Team is a team of highly trained volunteers and equipment that come together for International Search & Rescue missions. 

The team responds rapidly to humanitarian emergencies including earthquakes, floods and wildfires internationally on behalf of the Foreign Commonwealth Development Office (FCDO).

UK ISAR is formed from 14 of UK's Fire & Rescue Services, with support from volunteers in organisations outside of UK Fire & Rescue Services including dedicated medical teams,structural engineers, canine teams and vets.

What is UK ISAR?

Formed in 1992 UK ISAR established itself as the premier international search and rescue team, and official UK Government response through the Emergency Deployable Team program (EDT2) on behalf of and funded by the FCDO. UK ISAR is renowned for its expertise, dedication, and unwavering commitment to saving lives in the most challenging situations and speed to deploy across the globe ready to mobilize within a 6-10 hour time window from a designated National Point of Departure (NPOD).

UK ISAR team members are deployed through FCDO on behalf of the UK Government, following an international request for assistance from a disaster- affected country.

Name: NAMA Socialpreneur Relief
Description:
At NAMA, we are dedicated to rehabilitating and empowering individuals and families displaced by conflict. Through tailored capacity-building programs, we focus on developing entrepreneurial skills suited to basic self-sustained needs and subsistence. Our mission extends beyond economic empowerment, promoting mental well-being and resilience to ensure that those affected by war can rebuild their lives with dignity, self-reliance, and a healthy mind for long-term personal and community growth.

NAMA provides essential community services for capacity building after and during conflict, focusing on skill development for job creation and market access of disadvantageous groups and communities. NAMA plan of action will be focusing on rehabilitation, capacity building, entrepreneurial development, and mental well-being for families and individuals. NAMA will also work on facilitating vocational training programs to empower individuals and stimulate local economies. Tailored vocational training will align with local labor market demands.

These programs will focus on enhancing both hard and soft skills, ensuring that displaced individuals are equipped with the necessary competencies to thrive in their chosen fields. By providing access to capacity building initiatives, individuals will gain confidence in their abilities, fostering a sense of agency and self-worth as they build their own communities and help in balanced and sustainable development of Lebanon areas.

Name: Wayne Township Memorial First Aid Squad
Description:
Established in 1943, the Wayne Township Memorial First Aid Squad, born out of necessity, has evolved into an efficient organization responding to 5,000+ annual calls. After the loss of eight lives in 1942, concerned citizens formed the squad, officially responding to its first call on September 1, 1944. Headquartered on Oak Street, it expanded to Boonton Road, then Mountain View building on Taylor Drive, with additional facilities on Hamburg Turnpike.

The squad, which started with a 1942 Buick hearse, now operates five ambulances, a heavy rescue, gator, mobile ambulance bus, mass casualty trailers, chief cars, and two boats. Over 65 trained Emergency Medical Technicians (EMTs) ensure top-notch service. The Youth Squad, established in 1973, evolved into the Cadet Corps. Recognized as the Outstanding Volunteer EMS Agency in 2007, the squad's journey reflects growth, modernization, and dedicated service to the community. In 2016, it answered over 5,070 calls, showcasing its commitment to excellence and efficient emergency response.

At EvacTracker, our mission is simple: save lives through innovation.

Værdier: 
Vi tror på:

* Respekt for andre og sig selv. 
* Frihed til at træffe egne og selvstændige valg. 

Biler Til Ukraine indsamler midler til indkøb af primært 4x4 biler. Bilerne køres af frivillige chauffører fra Danmark til Ukraine, hvor de overdrages til vores samarbejdspartner. Bilerne bliver brugt ved fronten til civil undsætning, ambulancetjeneste, logistikopgaver, troppetransport etc. Historien: I forbindelse med Ruslands annektering af Krim i 2014 påbegyndtes en indsamling med henblik på at hjælpe og støtte Ukraine. Jesper Lindholt, intitiativtager og bosiddende i Kiev, og hans bror Morten, bosiddende i Lystrup ved Århus, forespurgte dengang i Ukraine, hvad der umiddelbart var størst behov for. Behovet for biler var og er stadig enormt. Det er begrundelsen for, at BtU fokuserer på levering af biler. 2 år inde i den fulde invasion har BtU leveret næsten 400 biler. ,

Avec Pool Hotline, vous obtenez l’aide dont vous avez besoin pour entretenir, réparer ou gérer votre piscine.
Des ressources expertes, une équipe à votre écoute et des solutions adaptées pour profiter de votre piscine toute l’année, en toute sérénité.

Name: Quick Response
Description: At Quick Response, community comes first. We're dedicated to supporting those in need through initiatives like our long-standing Christmas Lights display and partnerships with local charities such as Toys for Tots and Special Olympics. Throughout the year, we host family-friendly events to foster connection and camaraderie, because we believe in being there for our community year-round, not just during times of disaster.

Name: CommAlert
Description:
The CommAlert Group is an Indigenous-owned, 24/7 emergency communications provider. For over 25 years, our highly trained technicians, proven processes, and advanced technology have helped protect people, property, and communities... anytime, anywhere in the world.

We are skilled in crisis response, crisis communications, emergency dispatching, lone worker safety monitoring, journey management, GPS tracking, work-alone apps and hardware, and real-time alerts. We deliver live emergency call answering and dispatching, lone worker safety solutions with GPS tracking, and emergency alerts via voice, SMS, email, and push notifications.

Contact us to explore a custom safety solution for your organization.

Name: Prince William County Department of Fire and Rescue
Description:

The County’s Department of Fire and Rescue currently consists of 722 members, a combination of civilian and uniformed personnel.

Follow us on instagram @pwcfireandrescue

Name: St. Vincent de Paul Society of St. Joseph County
Description:
The St. Vincent de Paul Society of St. Joseph County, Inc. is a non-profit organization serving people in poverty in Northern Indiana. Our programs are intended to relieve emergencies, to provide support, and to encourage self-sufficiency. Our service is centered around honoring the dignity of the human person. We strive to see the face of Christ in everyone we serve and in each other.

We believe in joining together our staff, volunteers, donors, and partners in order to tackle poverty in our community. Together, we work to empower those we serve to help them move from a state of food and housing insecurity to a state of self-sufficiency and success.

Name: Servizio Autoambulanza Mendrisiotto
Description:
Il SAM è un servizio senza scopo di lucro che interviene 24/24 ore per qualsiasi tipo di necessità sanitaria, sia essa un infortunio, una malattia, una richiesta di trasferimento o l’assistenza durante manifestazioni sportive.

Nel complesso Sistema di Emergenza Sanitaria del Canton Ticino, il SAM rappresenta uno dei 5 Servizi Ambulanza del territorio ticinese. Ha come competenza primaria l’area del Mendrisiotto e Basso Ceresio per un totale di 16 Comuni e oltre 53’000 abitanti, a cui si aggiungono, nelle fasce orarie lavorative, circa 30’000 lavoratori pendolari non residenti. 
Le chiamate di soccorso della nostra popolazione sono raccolte, processate e assegnate al SAM dalla Centrale di Allarme e Coordinamento Sanitario Ticino Soccorso 144.
Anche il SAM, nella sua valenza regionale, è un sistema complesso e articolato in diverse funzioni, tutte finalizzate a quella che è la nostra missione, ossia il Soccorso Sanitario di Urgenza ed Emergenza le cui parole d’ordine sono Passione, Dedizione, Qualità, Efficienza, Efficacia.
Al SAM operano in maniera sinergica diverse figure professionali, la cui base comune è ovviamente una formazione sanitaria articolata in diverse discipline e specializzazioni, che a loro volta si integrano, si modulano e si compensano al fine di rispondere nel migliore dei modi ai diversi gradi di bisogno di salute della popolazione.

L’attività principale del SAM è il Soccorso Sanitario Urgente, che si articola fondamentalmente in due tipologie di intervento:

Soccorso Primario. Intervento svolto direttamente nel luogo in cui si manifesta il bisogno di cure, ad esempio abitazione, strada, uffici ed esercizi pubblici, impianti lavorativi e strutture sportive.
Soccorso Secondario. Intervento volto al trasferimento di un paziente da una struttura ospedaliera di ricovero a un’altra, sia per cure specialistiche sia per il rientro dopo l’erogazione delle stesse.

Name: Carlos Felipe Treinamentos
Description:
Que tal seria se todo mundo, além de adotar um comportamento seguro no seu dia a dia, soubesse o que fazer diante de uma emergência? Além de uma comunidade mais segura, o número de mortes evitáveis iria diminuir.

Após oito anos atuando como bombeiro em indústria e plantas de grande porte, decidi reunir todo o meu conhecimento, desenvolvido na prática e em diversos cursos e certificações, para tornar empresas, comunidades e pessoas preparadas para situações de emergência por meio do ensino de primeiros socorros.

Além do desenvolvimento de treinamentos específicos para cada necessidade, produzo e-books e conteúdos sobre o tema para a internet.

Vamos conversar e tornar seu ambiente de trabalho mais seguro?

Name: DebrisTech
Description: We help communities recover after natural disasters by monitoring and documenting debris removal for maximum FEMA reimbursement.

Name: Sécurité publique de la Ville de Québec
Description:
À Québec, nous sommes engagés à assurer un environnement sûr et sécuritaire pour les citoyens. Notre mission :  protéger, informer et sensibiliser la population, car la sécurité est une responsabilité partagée entre la Ville, ses citoyens et tous les acteurs du milieu.

La sécurité publique de la Ville de Québec, c’est :
- Le Service de police (SPVQ), qui veille à la protection de la vie et des biens, au maintien de l’ordre et à la prévention du crime.
- Le Service de protection contre l’incendie (SPCIQ), dédié à la prévention, l’intervention et la gestion des risques liés aux incendies.
- Le Bureau de la sécurité civile, qui veille à ce que tous soient en mesure de réagir de manière appropriée en cas de sinistre, tant sur le plan de la prévention, que de la préparation, de l’intervention et du rétablissement. 

Ensemble, ces services collaborent étroitement pour répondre aux besoins de la communauté et garantir une sécurité publique optimale.

Name: Shenzhen Thaistone Precision Hardware Co., Ltd.
Description:
THE THAISTONE ADVANTAGE
When it comes to being the best, Thaistone is driven by passion, precision and performance. Passion to establish a higher standard of design and set a new benchmark in diving equipment; precision to craft distinctive first-in-category products from the finest materials and manufacturing processes; and performance to deliver a superior experience and unwavering reliability for Thaistone divers.

BEFORE THAISTONE THERE WAS NO “BEST” IN SCUBA DIVING
Why be anything but the best? It’s a question that was asked whenThaistone was founded at the dawn of the 21st century—born to set new standards of excellence and innovation for superior materials and to simplify and enhance the diving experience.

A FULL RANGE OF DIVE EQUIPMENT OF THE HIGHEST CALIBER
We start by identifying a diver’s needs and desires, then we engineer the technology, materials and ergonomics to achieve maximum performance. Our design philosophy moves freely between pencil sketches and advanced CAD technology to achieve a superior product. Every Thaistone product design starts with the careful selection of the perfect material. And we don’t let cost or manufacturing difficulty limit us.

Name: Confined Space Rescue Service (CSRS)
Description:
Confined Space Rescue Services (CSRS)* are Technical Rescue Responders  and Consulting Safety Engineers.  We are based at the Port of Oakland, and provide onsite confined space training, entry, and rescue services since 1999.   We sincerely thank all our customers for your continued service!

Scope: 
High angle rope, and confined space  rescue and consulting.  Our team provides emergency services to governmental agencies, and privately held firms.

Consulting services include confined space assessments, reclassification, hazard assessments, rescue plans, and entry team training.  Team members are active/retired fire service and EH&S professionals.

Team members hold California State Fire Marshal (OES) Qualifications in Confined Space Rescue and Hazardous Materials Response.

* A division of SAFETRAN Site Safety Support Services, environmental health and safety consultants.

Name: Fundacja Rodziny Maj im. Piotra Maja
Description:
Dlaczego istniejemy?
By stworzyć przestrzeń, w której każdy czuje się bezpiecznie i pewnie. Naszą misją jest ułatwianie pomagania, bo choć pomoc bywa trudna, kryje się w niej ogromna siła. Pragniemy wspierać, motywować i inspirować.

Pomagamy potrzebującym, niosąc im realne wsparcie.
Budujemy społeczność gotową do wzajemnej pomocy.
Inspirujemy do zmiany stereotypowego postrzegania biznesu.

Nasza Fundacja działa na rzecz tworzenia społeczności opartej na tym, co najpiękniejsze: empatii i współpracy. Chcemy, by każdy miał możliwość skutecznego i bezpośredniego wsparcia tych, którzy tego najbardziej potrzebują.

Name: Palm Beach County Fire Rescue
Description:
Palm Beach County Fire Rescue is a Class #1 ALL HAZARDS Fire & Rescue Department and one of the largest in the State of Florida. Our Firefighter/Paramedics respond to just about every emergency you can imagine - fire, medical, car accidents, hazardous materials or conditions, plane crashes, aircraft emergencies, chemical spills, fuel spills, fire and arson investigations, water rescues, technical rescues, animal rescues, hurricane preparation, and response, and more!

Quick Statistics on PBCFR:

154,024 911 calls for help each year
51 fire stations, including Palm Beach International Airport
Covering nearly 1800 square miles
1810 employees
ISO Class 1 Department

During FY 2022, the The Insurance Services Office (ISO), Public Protection Classification, completed its analysis of the structural fire suppression delivery system of PBCFR. The rigorous process has resulted in PBCFR proudly joining the nation's elite group of fire rescue departments with its new Class 1 PPC rating.

Residents and businesses within the unincorporated areas and municipalities served by PBCFR can enjoy more safety and peace of mind knowing they have the best fire and life safety protection available. Class 1 rated areas typically offer lower insurance premiums for residents and businesses.

The ISO rates fire departments from 1-10 based on their ability to “respond to and prevent fires" with 1 being the best rating. The Class 1 rating applies to properties located within five road miles of a fire station.

Our Mission:

Name: Bridge Assistance
Description:
Une approche innovante de l’assistance !

Fondée avec la vision de révolutionner le paysage des assurances en Algérie, Bridge Assist est une société d'assistance dédiée à améliorer l'expérience des assurés. Pionniers dans le domaine de l'assistance du futur, nous intégrons des innovations majeures pour offrir de nouvelles possibilités, comme le suivi médical, la détection de problèmes à domicile et la gestion de la perte de bagages.

Bridge Assist : l'assistance du futur, déjà à vos côtés!

Name: Trinity Emergency Services
Description:
At Trinity Emergency Services (TES), we are dedicated to providing exceptional emergency services and state-of-the-art equipment to ensure the safety and well-being of our communities and a speedy return to “business as usual” for our clients. 

With a deep-rooted commitment to public service, we strive to be a reliable and trusted partner in times of crisis.

TES offers a comprehensive range of emergency services designed to address a wide array of situations. From medical emergencies to natural disasters and everything in between, our highly trained and experienced team is prepared to respond swiftly and effectively. We prioritize the preservation of life and property, acting with urgency and professionalism in every situation.

Equipped with cutting-edge technology and specialized equipment, we are at the forefront of emergency response capabilities and understand the importance of collaboration and coordination during critical moments. That’s why we work closely with other agencies, including law enforcement, hospitals, and local authorities, to foster seamless communication and enhance overall emergency response. Together, we form a united front, ready to tackle any challenge that comes our way.

Name: CEREF-Canungra Emergency Research and Educational Facility
Description: "Canungra Emergency Research and Education Facility (CEREF) is a world-class international emergency search and rescue training facility, bringing together First Responders globally to train at the one-stop-shop full-service, accredited, emergency training facility. Training opportunities provide real-life and environmental scenarios, covering all manner of weather conditions and situations typically experienced in natural disasters. State-of-the-art technology, equipment and facilities combine to service a range of global businesses and industries, the full suite of emergency services, defence forces and students year-round, incorporating day and night operations."

Name: Ownsafe
Description:
We're [Ownsafe], and we are the premier roadside assistance app.

When you're in the middle of nowhere and your car breaks down, we want to be there for you. We know that when you need help—and when no one is around—you can't just hope for the best. That's why our app is here: to provide users with everything they need to get back on the road quickly and safely.

Our app helps users find nearby services they can trust, as well as a list of all local mechanics so they don't have to worry about finding one on their own. We also have a feature that lets users take advantage of our own tow service if they're unable to find anyone else who will lend a hand. It's all available in one convenient place!

Pour vous libérer de ces contraintes et vous recentrer sur l'essentiel, nous avons développé avec des usagers et des professionnels  un prototype " Objectif Autonomie". 
La plateforme recense l'ensemble des aides de droits communs et dispositifs (établissements, services) à destination des personnes en situation de perte d'autonomie ponctuelle ou durable et de leurs aidants.

Description: Local Emergency Managers Alliance

Name: The Sunflower Project
Description: The Sunflower Project delivers emergency relief aid to the people of Ukraine. We are a charity relief organization, established in the United States as a 501c3 Nonprofit Corp and in Poland and Ukraine as a charitable foundation. The project was created by Kevin Raison and Rick Wilson within a few days of the Russian invasion of Ukraine in 2022. We raise money and support in the US, and use it to purchase and deliver food, clothing, medical supplies, animal feed, building materials, and other humanitarian aid.  While delivering aid remains a core activity of the project, we have largely shifted our focus to de-mining activities in the liberated territories of Ukraine. This project is about the very real and tangible ways that Americans can stand with Ukrainians. Our goal is to demine, resupply and rebuild Ukraine.

Name: SceneSafe BioDecon | Biohazard Mitigation and Remediation
Description:
SceneSafe BioDecon is an Austin-based biohazard mitigation company owned and operated by firefighters in Central Texas. We specialize in the cleanup of homes and businesses impacted by traumatic events such as suicide, violent crime, medical emergencies, and unwitnessed death. 

Our mission is to restore properties to a safe, habitable condition so that our clients can focus on what matters most during difficult times. 

As firefighters, our core mission is to protect lives and property from a broad range of threats including fires, medical emergencies, roadway accidents, hazardous materials incidents, and severe weather. As a result, we’ve often seen firsthand the devastating impact that a death, violent crime, or other emergency can have on family, friends, and employers. 

What surprises many people is that once emergency responders leave, cleanup of the scene becomes the responsibility of the home or property owner. The scope of this cleanup can be substantial, often involving blood, bodily fluids, and other potentially infectious materials. For families trying to navigate complex legal and emotional matters, figuring out where to start can feel overwhelming.

That’s why we started SceneSafe—to act as the ‘second responders’ who step in once the first responders have left. We take pride in our ability to restore homes and businesses to a safe, habitable condition, allowing for a sense of normalcy to return to our clients' lives so they can focus on other pressing concerns. Our top priority is to ensure the safety of our clients, their families, and our technicians throughout every step of the remediation process.

Name: EU HNS: Host Nation Support
Description:
Host Nation Support project with the development of an HNS toolkit, an online course and table-top exercises in the Republic of Moldova and the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan, funded by the European Union.
Each TTX is conducted in cooperation with the national CP authorities in the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan, (Public Security Directorate, Civil Defence Directorate) and the Republic of Moldova (General Inspectorate for Emergency Situations, Ministry of Internal Affairs, Republic of Moldova) will have a capacity of a minimum of twenty participants. These are consisting of key personnel of disaster management stakeholders, Civil Protection Authorities, and line Ministries at a national level as per the national emergency response framework based in the host nation.

Name: Lincolnshire Resilience Forum
Description:
Lincolnshire Resilience Forum (LRF) is designed to help people prepare for, respond to, and recover from major emergencies or incidents. LRFs exist across the entire country and are made up of expert partner organisations working together from the public and private sectors within defined regions aligned with Police Force areas. In Lincolnshire the boundary of responsibility covers a total of 2,292 miles and a population of over 750,000. Local Resilience Forums are activated to assist in responding to  large-scale emergencies when a combined multi-agency coordination would result in a more effective and efficient outcome.  Anyone of the partner agencies can also activate the LRF when there is a requirement for more capacity beyond their own resources.
Emergency situations that may require action from an LRF include severe weather events, coastal or inland flooding, acts of terrorism or other sudden dangerous situations which seriously threaten people’s welfare, the environment or our security.
Local Resilience Forums develop, implement and coordinate multi-agency responses and action plans focussed on;
●	Saving lives
●	Relieving suffering
●	Protecting property
●	Preventing further damage to the environment
●	Preventing the impacts of a disaster getting worse
●	Restoring normality as quickly as possible
Lincolnshire Resilience Forum aims to inform families, communities and businesses on how they can prepare themselves for large-scale, major emergencies in Lincolnshire. 
Lincolnshire Resilience Forum aims to inform families, communities and businesses on how they can prepare themselves for large-scale, major emergencies in Lincolnshire. The close collaboration of LRF partners is not limited to responding to emergencies, and dealing with the aftermath, it also includes a continuous programme of behind the scenes activity in planning, preparation and development of response strategies for potential emergency risks in the county.

Name: Casualties Union

Description:

Name: Living Lab Neurochirurgie
Description:
Welkom bij Living Lab Neurochirurgie. Een unieke organisatie met professionals vanuit de acute zorg binnen het ziekenhuis, ambulance, brandweer, politie en defensie. Bij het leveren van effectieve traumazorg is het van cruciaal belang om verschillende disciplines en perspectieven te combineren. Defensie heeft jarenlange ervaring met acute traumazorg binnen oorlog- en conflictsituaties, terwijl zorgprofessionals op de vloer hun expertise hebben van de specifieke uitdagingen en beperkingen in de civiele gezondheidszorg. Door deze kennis en ervaring te combineren, kunnen we de beste resultaten bereiken voor patiënten. Samenwerking tussen professionals vanuit de acute zorg binnen het ziekenhuis, ambulance, brandweer, politie en defensie kan leiden tot innovatieve en effectieve oplossingen voor de acute traumazorg binnen Nederland. 

Het afgelopen jaar heeft Living Lab Neurochirurgie zich tevens ingezet voor de slachtoffers in het oorlogsgebied Gaza. Hierbij hebben we voorzien in voedselpakketten, waterrantsoenen, warme maaltijden, tenten als onderkomen en medische hulpmiddelen. Naast deze vorm van acute hulpverlening in de primaire basisbehoeften hebben we er voor gezorgd dat we een poule aan medische hulpverleners hebben klaarstaan die bereid zijn om medische hulp in Gaza te gaan verlenen zodra er een staakt-het-vuren is afgekondigt. Daarbij zijn deze teams voorzien van medische apparatuur en communicatie middelen. Wilt u dit initiatief ook steunen? Doneer dan via: https://bunq.me/noodhulpgaza

Name: Global Disaster Logistics, LLC

Whether you're in need of restroom or shower trailers, mobile sleep trailers, full-service catering, waste management, industrial tents, or power, we deliver the resources you need, wherever and whenever you need them.

With 24/7 nationwide deployment and turnkey base camp services, we’re committed to ensuring your operation runs smoothly, even in the most challenging conditions.

Alfa Asistans,
Türkiye’nin dört bir yanında kesintisiz hizmet sunan öncü bir şirkettir. 81 ilde faaliyet gösteren firmamız, sigorta şirketlerine, otomotiv markalarına, yetkili satıcılara ve araç kiralama şirketlerine asistans, garanti ve denetim hizmetleri sağlamaktadır.

Uzman ekibimiz, sektördeki en deneyimli ve yetenekli profesyonellerden oluşur. Müşteri odaklı yaklaşımımızla, her zaman en iyi ve en hızlı çözümleri sunmaya çalışıyoruz.

Firmamız, çalışanlarımızın motivasyonunu ve iş verimliliğini artırmak amacıyla sağladığı en önemli avantajı, 4G destekli tablet PC'ler aracılığıyla, ev veya ofis dışında, yerel internet altyapısına bağımlı olmadan istedikleri her yerde çalışma imkanı sunmaktadır. Bu nedenle, 7 gün 24 saat boyunca ulaşılabiliriz ve her türlü acil durumda yanınızdayız. Size güvenilir, profesyonel ve zamanında hizmet sunarak, sektörde öne çıkan bir marka olma misyonumuzu sürdürüyoruz.

Garantili hizmet ve geniş bir servis ağımız sayesinde, müşterilerimizin ihtiyaçlarını her zaman karşılayabiliyoruz. Alfa Asistans olarak, her zaman müşterilerimizin yanında olmaktan gurur duyuyoruz ve güvenilir bir iş ortağı olmayı taahhüt ediyoruz.

Name: International Humanitarian Partnership
Description: The International Humanitarian Partnership (IHP) serves as a platform for international collaboration, pooling resources—both equipment and personnel—to bolster the collective response capacity of its member organizations. Its mission is to support affected populations by enhancing the response efforts of humanitarian actors, primarily the United Nations, as well as other organizations and the European Union, in addressing natural disasters and complex emergencies, including conflict-related crises. This is accomplished by offering tailored operational and technical support to multilateral organizations involved in humanitarian aid worldwide.

Name: MedEvac
Description: Med Evac is available 24 hours a day, 365 days a year, providing on-scene emergency response and inter-facility transport. Med Evac is dedicated to the highest level of patient care. By strategically locating aircraft throughout north and eastern Colorado, the time to reach patients is significantly decreased. This is especially noted in trauma, STEMI and stroke patients, with many positive outcomes that can be directly related to a shorter time before the patient reaches definitive care.

Name: LifeCare Medical Transports
Description:
LifeCare Medical Transports began operations in 1994 in Fredericksburg, Virginia, and has grown from a local service to one of Virginia’s largest health care transportation providers. Our commitment to quality services is supported by modern medical care, compassion for personalized care and concern for the community we serve. At LifeCare, we place great importance on our customer’s needs. Of primary importance are services that meet all of our patient’s health care transportation requirements.
Corporate Offices and a Centralized Communications Center are maintained where dispatch, billing services and administrative functions are completed. Licensed at the highest level of service recognized by the Commonwealth of Virginia, Department of Health – Office of Emergency Medical Services, LifeCare functions at the Advanced Life Support (ALS) level to ensure a broad and complete range of services for our customers.
LifeCare provides essential emergency, non-emergency, Basic Life Support (BLS), Advanced Life Support (ALS), Specialty Care Transport (SCT/Critical Care) and other varied services throughout the Commonwealth of Virginia. Our reputation as a health care transportation leader has been earned by the dedicated commitment of our team members. Their daily efforts represent and reinforce our mission to provide a commitment to the highest quality services. LifeCare has a fleet of over 135 ambulances and employs over 500 employees with both health care and business training with community based locations throughout Virginia and Maryland.
With an innovative approach to many processes, LifeCare incorporates modern technology with day-to- day operations. Developing and implementing programs and process improvements created by a team-based environment represents our desire to not only stay on the cutting edge of patient care, but to lead the way.
LifeCare was the first commercial ambulance company to have received the Virginia Governor’s Award for Outstanding EMS Agency.

Name: DRK-Kreisverband Freiburg e.V.
Description: We are one of 16 district chapters inside the Baden Red Cross. As an association of independent welfare services, a national aid society and with an independend youth chapter, the Freiburg chapter and all its branches are part of the national and international Red Cross and Red Crescant movement. Our area of activity extends beyond the city of Freiburg and across the entire Breisgau-Hochschwarzwald district in the southern Black Forest in the southwest of Germany.

Name: The Thunderbird Project
Description:
Our Mission:
Enabling Emergency Service drones to operate Beyond Visual Line of Sight (BVLOS), providing life-critical support.

Our Vision:
The world has witnessed incredible advances in drone capability over recent years. However, there are vital areas of emergency response where this technology could deliver more: providing invaluable situational awareness to mission commanders, helping to locate an increasing number of high-risk missing persons, or delivering medical aid to casualties more quickly than conventional means – saving time where every minute counts. 
There’s no doubt that eventually all these capabilities will develop, but this project is founded on the passionate belief that by bringing emergency services together through a multi-agency, systems approach, with no commercial or institutional bias, we can accelerate this journey – saving more lives, sooner and with few resources.

Visit our website for more details.

The Thunderbird Project is supported by independent consultancies Ajuno and Frazer-Nash, who provide technical strategy and systems integration.

Name: PUERTO EMERGENCIAS
Description:
PUERTO EMERGENCIAS comenzó sus actividades en el año 2009 en la localidad de Puerto General San Martin. Un grupo de profesionales de la Emergencia médica extraprehospitalaria, reunidos por convicción, por vocación, por amistad, por objetivos y sueños en común, formaron un sistema de emergencias como ellos los soñaron. Un servicio con el recurso humano capacitado y entrenado específicamente para asistir a situaciones de emergencias.
Luego de algunos años de desarrollo la zona de cobertura se expandió por el cordón industrial por las localidades de San Lorenzo, Fray Luis Beltrán, Capitán Bermudez, Granadero Baigorria, 
En el año 2014 Puerto Emergencias desembarcó en la ciudad de Rosario, motivo de gran expectativa para sus fundadores ya que los mismos son rosarinos,  con la experiencia y solvencia necesaria para brindar un servicio de excelencia a sus afiliados y clientes, basándose en la capacitación continua de su personal , en el avance tecnológico de los recursos físicos, siempre recordando los mismos valores y objetivos que movilizaron a este grupo de profesionales a formar Puerto Emergencias.

Description:
YAKKUM Emergency Unit (YEU) is one of YAKKUM (Christian Foundation for Public Health) units which was established in 2001. YEU is a community-based humanitarian organization working in various regions across Indonesia. YEU has been assisting communities in facing disasters and climate change with an approach that is inclusive, value-based, and sustainable.
YEU is present not only to respond to emergencies but also to build long-term systems and capacities that enable communities to recover and lead their own recovery. YEU combines local knowledge with context-appropriate innovation and encourages leadership from within the community.

Name: MedSTAR Transport
Description:
MedSTAR Transport is a CAMTS-accredited transport program that transfers about 6,000 patients yearly to and from MedStar Health facilities throughout the Baltimore and greater Washington, D.C., area. Our fleet includes both state-of-the-art medevac helicopters and ambulances. And thanks to our highly qualified air and ground transportation teams, no patient is far from advanced care.

All of our patients are transported between hospitals in the MedStar Health system and other major tertiary care centers in the Mid-Atlantic region as directed by the sending and receiving physicians. Medical control physicians are immediately available 24 hours a day for consultation with referring physicians and flight crews.

During transport, our specialized team can initiate or continue critical care via our current evidence-based treatment protocols in any setting and have immediate access to critical care physicians. Our teams routinely manage patients on advanced mechanical ventilation modes including high-flow nasal cannula and APRV, Intra-Aortic Balloon Pumps, Ventricular Assist Devices, Impellas, ECMO, and many more advanced therapies.

Learn more: https://www.medstarhealth.org/services/medstar-transport

Name: Rescue1Cy
Description:
The Cyprus Emergency Response Team RESCUE-1 is a non-profit voluntary rescue organization founded in 2018 based in Limassol.Its members are volunteer ready 24/7 to provide their services when and where needed.


First Aid
First aid knowledge is a timeless need for human. It is even more important nowadays where modern lifestyles and technology have multiplied the dangers surrounding us. For a volunteer rescuer, first aid knowledge is a prerequisite. Extensive multi-level training by experienced trainers and health professionals ensures our rescuers' excellent ability to effectively support the patient's life until the arrival of medical help.

Rescue of missing persons
From time to time, the Police are requesting volunteers to identify missing persons. Methodology and systematicity in conducting research ensures early identification and safe rescue of the missing person with minimal health consequences.

Volunteer Rescuers in forest fires
Forest fires are a major problem in the countries of the Mediterranean, which are trying to improve not only the effectiveness but also the efficiency of managing this phenomenon / risk. Good training of our volunteers and appropriate equipment is an additional important tool for achieving this goal.

Operation weather Rescuers

Earthquake
Cyprus is located in the second largest earthquake zone of the planet, which is experiencing earthquakes, which account for around 15% of global seismic activity. The assistance of trained volunteer rescuers will achieve a faster rescue of surface wounded and will ensure timely identification and rescue of trapped ruined people.

Floods
Severe weather phenomenon occasionally causes catastrophic floods from which properties as well as human lives are at risk. Well-trained and appropriately equipped volunteer rescuers is an additional factor in the efforts of state agencies to manage these incidents.

Name: Morgan Chase Management
Description:
At MCM SOLUTIONS, we specialize in delivering high-quality meal services in even the most challenging field environments. With over three years of experience, our dedicated team has successfully prepared and served more than 21 million meals and nearly 10 million snacks in support of emergency base camps and missions across the continental U.S., U.S. territories, the Caribbean, and South America.

We are equipped with custom-designed assets, including mobile kitchens, preparation units, portable walk-in coolers, and high-volume dishwashing trailers. This allows MCM to efficiently set up temporary cafeterias, mess halls, and field kitchens in remote locations. Our extensive inventory of in-house food service equipment enables us to tailor our operations to meet the unique needs of each deployment. With seven brick-and-mortar and nine mobile kitchens, we are capable of producing up to 87,000 meals daily across the continental U.S. Moreover, our containerized kitchens can serve up to 40,000 meals per day in international settings, while our skid kitchens allow us to scale meal preparation in emergencies.

Safety is our highest priority when serving meals in remote areas. The MCM team adheres to strict Food Safety and HACCP guidelines that meet or exceed USDA, DOD, and ServSafe standards. Our site services team goes above and beyond to ensure a secure environment, successfully minimizing the risk of injury with only two work-related incidents in the past decade. We take immense pride in maintaining safe operations, even in challenging conditions.

No matter your needs—whether it's Meals Ready-to-Eat (MREs), simple box lunches, meal delivery to multiple locations, or full-scale industrial kitchen capabilities—MCM SOLUTIONS can adapt our operations to fit both small projects and high-volume demands. We pride ourselves on our swift response times, ranging from 6 to 48 hours, depending on mission requirements and circumstances.

We are committed to excellence in quality!

Name: Canadian Medical Assistance Teams
Description:
CMAT is entirely volunteer-run and donor-driven. In order to keep our expenses to a minimum, we have no paid employees.  Volunteers are welcome to apply at www.cmat.ca 


VISION
Through its medical relief and development projects, CMAT seeks to improve the health and welfare of families both in Canada and in developing countries around the world.

MISSION STATEMENT
By preserving human dignity, helping to reduce suffering and building capacity, CMAT aims to assist vulnerable communities most affected by poverty, conflict and natural disaster to heal their biological, psychological, and social wounds.  

VALUES AND CODE OF CONDUCT
Through its projects, CMAT ratifies the Humanitarian Charter signed originally in 1997, and aspires to attain the standards determined by the Sphere Project, which are based on two core beliefs:

a) All possible steps should be taken to alleviate human suffering arising out of calamity and conflict; and

b) Those affected by disaster have a right to life with dignity and therefore a right to assistance.

Moreover, CMAT strives to adhere to the International Code of Conduct and to the humanitarian principles of humanity, impartiality, neutrality, and solidarity in the implementation of its medical relief and development projects.

Name: Al-Ayn Almennyttig Fond DK
Description:
Al-Ayn Almennyttig Fond DK er den danske afdeling tilknyttet Al-Ayns hovedkontor som er i Irak. Al-Ayn er den største nødhjælpsorganisation i Irak, hvor vi fokuserer på at gøre livet nemmere for forældreløse børn. Vi har således over 99.000 forældreløse børn under vores paraply. Vi har fire eksisterende centre i Irak, og 11 centre under opførelse. 
I disse centre giver vi de forældreløse børn læge- og psykologhjælp og vi tillærer dem erhvervskompetencer, så de får redskaberne til at klare sig selv. 

Vi tilbyder ligeledes sponsorordninger så hr. og fru Danmark kan sponsorere et forældreløst barn for omkring 500 kroner om måneden. 

Vi er en professionel non-profit organisation, så dine donationer går 100% ubeskåret til børnene. 

For os at se, så er det vigtigt at støtte de forældreløse børn i et samfund af flere grunde. 
Først og fremmest har børn uden en forsørger ringe forudsætninger for at få et godt liv i et samfund fyldt med tragedier og fattigdom. I Irak bor fem procent af alle verdens forældreløse børn. Denne situation skaber selvsagt en stor byrde for resten af samfundet.

Derfor kan man ved at hjælpe ét barn både redde barnets liv, styrke lokalsamfundets sammenhængskraft og derigennem få resten af samfundet til at spire. På den måde reducerer man antallet af forældreløse børn og forebygger, at man som barn dømmes til elendighed, hvis der skulle ske noget med forældrene.

Vi glæder os til at vise jer vores arbejde og forhåbentlig få jer med om bord, når vi skal give forældreløse børn et bedre liv!

Name: Disaster Response Communications & Information Technology (DRCIT)
Description:
DRCIT provides portable communications and technology equipment in preparation for, during, and following disasters and emergencies to the general public, NGOs/NPOs, and first responders. This includes providing volunteers to handle/relay communications for first responders, hospitals, and NGOs/NPOs; providing equipment (e.g. radios/repeaters, satellite, wired/wireless network equipment, computers, etc.); training first responders on the use of alternative methods of communications in preparation for disasters, and more. We also ship our equipment to other agencies to support their response at no cost.  

We also provide specialized communication gear in the form of customized kits for use by hospitals, CERT teams, emergency communication teams, and the general public, making use of public safety, GMRS, amateur, marine, and commercial radio gear. 

All disaster relief work is supported by voluntary donations and grants.

Name: Joint Emergency Communications Center
Description:
The Joint Emergency Communications Center of Johnson County (JECC) is the clearing house for all 9-1-1 calls and all Emergency Communications for Johnson County. The JECC serves as the epicenter for emergency communications for all citizens of Johnson County. The Johnson County Emergency Management Agency (EMA) is also located in the same facility.

The JECC serves the public 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, and 365 days a year with specially trained dispatchers to answer all emergency calls for assistance. These operators receive and/or transmit emergency telephone, radio, alarm, and other types of data over multiple computer systems, then analyze it in a timely manner for the purpose of relaying the information between citizens and first responders during emergencies in order to preserve life and property.

Another important responsibility of Emergency Communications Personnel is the accurate entry and removal of wanted persons, articles and vehicles in NCIC. This includes providing, on demand, the results of NCIC queries made by field units.

Name: Rations Canada
Description:
Our Directive at Rations Canada is to combat future food insecurity and hyperinflation through the provision of strategic stockpiles of basic food staples that are packaged to last for 30+ years and can be stored in any climate. 

We believe that access to high-quality, nutrient-rich food is a fundamental human right, and we're committed to providing Canadians and global allies with the tools they need to achieve long-term food security.

Our team of experts has carefully selected a range of essential food staples, including grains, meats, fruits and vegetables, which are vital for any long-term food storage plan.

We understand that the world is changing rapidly, and we're committed to staying ahead of the curve to ensure that our community have the best possible tools for long-term food security.

Name: Emergency Food Planner
Description:
Deciding to provide safety and security for your family by storing some food for emergencies is easy.

Deciding how much you need, what types of food and managing the varying expiration dates is hard!

Our food planner is a privacy first, online family emergency food planner that helps you to prepare as much as you feel is reasonable for your family or community. Planning might be as simple as a weekend camping trip or as complex as an adverse event lasting 6 months or longer. You choose the goal – our planner helps you build the road map.

Name: WPI EMS
Description:
Worcester Polytechnic Institute EMS (WPI EMS) has been proudly serving WPI since 1975. We are a student-run organization that provides emergency care for on-campus emergencies and events. Name: Pett Level Independent Rescue Boat
Description:
PLIRB is a voluntary lifeboat service, serving the coastline and inland waters between Hastings and Rye. Our mission is the preservation of life at sea, on the coastline and inland waters.

We are not affiliated to RNLI or HM Coastguard but work in close collaboration with their local units as well as all other emergency services.

As one of approximately 75 independent lifeboat stations across the UK, we are entirely self-managed and self-funded. We receive no public or centralised funding – we raise funds through donations, fundraising activities, grant applications and our Sea Shanty group – the Pett Slip Buoys. All operational costs, development projects and boat/vehicle assets we have to work hard to secure funds for. Support 

We operate 24/7 and 365 days a year. We are formally tasked by UK Coastguard in the same way as RNLI services. We are extremely proud that our average launch time is 15-20 minutes from the time of paging. This is thanks to our local volunteer base who are on call day and night.

We operate two RIB boats, a 5.4metre Zodiac Pro and a 3.8metre Arancia surf RIB. These are the ideal assets to cope with the varied and hazardous coastline – we can navigate close under the cliffs, launch in low and high tide and cover the entire Rye Bay stretch.

We train twice a week, all year round. All volunteers receive a rigorous training programme and have the opportunity to undertake professional qualifications (radio license, powerboat courses, sea survival, casualty care and first aid). Volunteers can decide how they contribute – boat crew, beach/base crew, events and fundraising or administrative.

We welcome Duke of Edinburgh scheme students who spend several months training with us to learn vital skills, gain experience and build confidence.

Name: NIMS Logic
Description:
NIMS Logic is built for how incidents actually run.

Most teams feel the pain in operations and situational awareness, not in paperwork. NIMS Logic is designed around that reality: we support the Incident Management Team in the heat of the response, and only then turn that operational data into a complete reimbursement package.

No duplicate entry. No guessing what will be needed years later.

Phase 1: Operational Process Flow
 - Guides ICS roles through structured, repeatable workflows.
 - Ensures critical steps are not missed in Planning, Operations, Logistics, and Finance/Admin.
 - Captures decisions and rationale at the point of action, not after the fact.

Phase 2: Situational Awareness for Actionable Decisions
 - Provides a live, shared picture of what is happening and where constraints are.
 - Surfaces the right information to the right role at the right time.
 - Supports command staff in making faster, better-documented decisions.

Phase 3: Recovery and Reimbursement
 - Uses data captured in Phases 1 and 2 to automatically generate audit-ready documentation.
 - Aligns with FEMA requirements to reduce delays and deobligation risk.
 - Creates a defensible record that can be referenced for years after the incident.

Your Digital Coach

NIMS Logic acts as a digital coach for the Incident Management Team. It enforces discipline in process flow and improves situational awareness during the incident, automatically creating the disciplined record you need for efficient recovery.

We are partnering with forward-leaning emergency management and public safety organizations who want to modernize incident operations first, while also protecting their recovery dollars on the back end.

To request early access or learn more, visit https://nimslogic.com/ or email info@nimslogic.com.

Name: Croce Bianca Volpianese ODV
Description:
Trovarsi ad assistere se ben involontariamente, ad un incidente non è piacevole, ancor peggio è vedere soffrire una persona in attesa che arrivino i soccorsi. Nel 1979 non esisteva una Pubblica Assistenza a Volpiano dunque in caso di emergenza si doveva aspettare l’arrivo dell’ambulanza dai comuni limitrofi, spesso irrimediabilmente.
Stanchi di questa situazione, non con pochi sforzi, riuscimmo a fondare nel 1980 la “Croce Bianca Volpianese”.
Nello stesso anno dell’incidente, raggiungemmo già un buon numero di soci (40) ancor prima di possedere un’ambulanza.
Attualmente (2025) siamo 22 Dipendenti e 209 Volontari, con la speranza di poter crescere sempre di più.
Un ringraziamento speciale a tutti coloro che in questi anni hanno supportato, stimolato e aiutato la nostra associazione.

Name: Boundstone Group
Description:
Are you a firefighter, paramedic, coastguard or do you have ambitions to be one? You may work for an organisation with an emergency response function or be part of the local resilience or emergency management network. These roles inevitably face highly pressurised situations, where the ability to think clearly and to remain focused on the task in hand is essential. 

And that is where we can help. We were firefighters, we’ve been where you will be and we understand how performance can be impacted by pressure. Having served the public for over 40 years between us we now want to help those who do the job to do it to the absolute best of their ability. 

In moments of high stress you’ve probably been told to ‘stay calm’ or ‘don’t get tunnel vision’, but that advice never comes with a solution, until now.

Name: Stichting Proplan
Description:
Stichting Proplan helpt hen die geen helper hebben met hulp, hoop en toekomst. We willen middelen en mogelijkheden aanreiken voor de allerarmsten, ongeacht geloof, ras of etniciteit. 

U kent vast het verhaal van de Barmhartige Samaritaan: een reiziger die beroofd wordt, diverse mensen lopen voorbij, zien het gebeuren, maar niemand doet iets. Totdat er een persoon voorbij komt die wél hulp biedt. Een vreemdeling. Dit verhaal sluit direct aan bij onze missie: help hen die geen helper hebben. Het enige wat telt is of we in ons dagelijks leven omzien naar de mensen in nood, die op ons pad komen.

Al sinds 1997 werkt Stichting Proplan aan projecten voor de kanslozen en allerarmsten, zodat we hun negatieve spiraal helpen doorbreken. Samen met uw hulp zijn we actief in voormalig Joegoslavië en dat is niet zonder reden. Als gevolg van de burgeroorlog zijn veel hulporganisaties vertrokken, maar de nood is nog steeds erg hoog op tal van plekken, zoals bij de weduwen van Srebrenica, die eenzaam, depressief en zwaar getraumatiseerd zijn. De werkeloosheid is groot en de helft van de bevolking leeft onder de armoedegrens. Kinderen kunnen geen goede opleiding volgen en veel mensen hebben een slechte gezondheid door ongezonde voeding en gebrek aan medische zorg.

Onze no-nonsense aanpak, ons lokale netwerk en het zelf aanwezig zijn in de regio, maakt dat meer dan 95% van elke euro terecht komt op de plek waar deze het hardst nodig is.

Name: Haven
Description:
Haven emerged during GovHack AU 2024 with a vision to see a world where communities are better prepared and supported in the face of natural disasters and security challenges. By fostering collaboration and resilience, we aim to ensure no one faces an emergency alone.

The initial concept addresses emergency response to bushfires through an ecosystem that integrates advanced data, predictive analytics, AI, and a dynamic app and community platform. It also includes intuitive dashboards designed to provide government agencies with actionable insights for resource allocation, emergency response, and recovery efforts. These tools empower informed decision-making while connecting individuals and businesses to critical information and support networks during crises.

Our mission is to build stronger, safer communities through innovation and collaboration.

Name: Amdox Virtual ED
Description:
Welcome to the Amdox Virtual Emergency page, your gateway to innovative telemedicine solutions in the field of emergency medicine. Our mission is to revolutionize how patients receive critical medical care, anytime and anywhere.

 Emergency Medicine: The Dynamic Frontline
Discover the pivotal role emergency medicine plays in providing immediate medical attention to patients facing acute injuries, severe illnesses, and life-threatening conditions. Our expert team is dedicated to delivering top-tier care in the moments that matter most.

 Transforming Care with Tele-Emergency
 
 Breaking Barriers, Saving Lives
 
 Advantages of Virtual Emergency Medicine:
 Swift Triage: Prioritize critical cases swiftly through teleconsultations.
 Resource Efficiency: Guide on-site staff remotely, optimizing resource allocation.
 Cost-Effective Care: Cut down on expenses with virtual consultations.
 Seamless Transitions: Facilitate continuity of care between facilities.
 Expert Insights: Access specialized consultants for enhanced diagnostics.
 Rural Reach: Extend critical care to underserved rural communities.
 Minimized Exposure: Safeguard against infectious diseases, anytime.
 Patient-Centric: Receive timely care from the comfort of your home.

 Non-Urgent Cases, Smart Solutions
Understanding that not all emergencies are life-threatening, we're here to provide smart solutions for non-urgent cases. Our integrated approach ensures appropriate care while relieving the pressure on traditional emergency departments.
 Notable Non-Urgent Cases:

Minor injuries and abrasions
Sprains, minor fractures, and burns
Mild allergic reactions
Common cold and flu symptoms
Earaches and minor infections
...and more.
 Empowering Healthcare for All
Join us in transforming emergency care delivery. Whether it's remote consultations, streamlined triage, or expert guidance, Amdox Virtual Emergency is here to elevate the standard of care for everyone.

Name: Fundación Pública Urxencias Sanitarias de Galicia-061
Description:
A Fundación Pública Urxencias Sanitarias de Galicia-061 naceu en 1995 co obxectivo de que os profesionais de urxencias achegasen a asistencia sanitaria ata o lugar onde se atopase o paciente e de que fosen os propios pacientes quen contactasen directamente co servizo público de emerxencias, marcando un sixelo número de télefono: 061.
Dende as primeiras oito ambulancias medicalizadas e as 75 ambulancias de soporte vital básico (SVB) para asistir en toda a Comunidade Autónoma galega fomos crecendo ata conformar unha Rede de transporte sanitario urxente formada por 17 ambulancias de soporte vital avanzado (SVA), 107 ambulancias de SVB e tres ambulancias convencionais; ademais de dous helicópteros medicalizados.
Como non podía ser doutro xeito, a evolución deste servizo foi posible grazas a cada un dos profesionais que co seu traballo diario posibilitan que o 061 de Galicia continúe sendo o servizo que máis valoran os seus usuarios dentro do Servizo Galego de Saúde e que nos converteramos para a cidadanía, en moitas ocasións, na porta de entrada ao sistema sanitario público.
En Urxencias Sanitarias de Galicia-061 camiñamos cara á mellora continua a través dun firme compromiso coa transparencia na xestión do servizo, a calidade na asistencia e a innovación permanente dos recursos materais e dos recursos humanos a través da formación continuada e especializada. Tendo sempre presente a misión do servizo de ofrecer ao cidadán a solición inmediata máis axeitada ao seu problema de saúde.
Para terminar, a través desta páxina web pretendemos achegarnos, aínda máis, a todos aqueles que xa nos conñecen, informando sobre o noso traballo e os nosos proxectos, e a aqueles que queren saber sobre nós, mostrando quen somos e como traballamos.

Name: PARATUS project
Description:
The PARATUS project aims at increasing the preparedness of first and second responders in the face of multi-hazard events and to reduce the risks related to impacts on various sectors that result from complex disasters. The outcome is to develop a cloud-based Online Service Platform that offers support in reducing dynamic risk scenarios and systemic vulnerability caused by multi-hazard disasters.
In order to achieve these objectives, the project will perform in-depth assessments of complex interactions between hazards and their resulting impacts in various sectors, as well as analyse the current risk situation and study how alternative future scenarios could change multi-hazard impact chains. Based on this analysis, scenarios of multi-hazard impacts will be co-designed with stakeholders and developed in four case study areas (including the Caribbean, Romania, Istanbul, and Alpine areas).

Visit the European funded network for Crisis Management, Resilient Societies, Safety & Security: https://www.cmine.eu

Name: FiSci
Description:
At FiSci, our vision is to transform wildfire management across the full lifecycle. We are focused on the critical challenge of mitigation and the prevention of catastrophic bushfires, providing advanced analytics to help organisations move from a reactive to a proactive stance on risk.

Our flagship platform, FiSci Mitigate, is a cloud-based strategic tool designed for bushfire risk analytics and mitigation planning. It empowers users to create projects for specific regions, analyse environmental conditions, run powerful simulations, and generate actionable insights to support planning, decision-making, and reporting.

Mitigate delivers three levels of powerful analytics:

* Descriptive: Understand your landscape with dynamic, fully customisable layers for vegetation, fire history, and fuel loads.

* Predictive: Model the future. Our platform predicts fuel load accumulation over time and runs sophisticated fire spread simulations under any weather scenario. Users can visualise "Risk From" (where the most dangerous fires start) and "Risk To" (which areas are most frequently impacted), complete with 3D playback and KPIs like fireline intensity and flame height.

* Prescriptive: Go beyond just identifying risk. Actively plan your response. Users can build, test, and compare multiple mitigation strategies (e.g., prescribed burns, mechanical thinning) against a "No Mitigation" baseline. This allows you to quantify the effectiveness of your treatments and even analyse their financial costs.

Mitigate is built for a wide range of organisations, including government agencies, utilities, infrastructure operators, land managers, and environmental organisations. Whether you are conducting development assessments, planning renewable energy projects, protecting forestry assets, or managing critical infrastructure, our platform provides the quantitative, evidence-based analysis needed for defensible, outcome-based planning.

For 25 years, the High Alert Institute has been a global leader in disaster preparedness, resilience, and sustainability. Our mission is to empower individuals, organizations, and communities to anticipate, withstand, and recover from disasters, creating a safer, more resilient world.

Our Vision
To empower individuals, families, communities, and organizations to achieve Resilience, Innovation, Sustainability, Equity, and Empathy—RISE² to every challenge.

Our Impact
•	Trained over 20,000 educators, responders, and community leaders.
•	Advanced health equity, literacy, and mental wellness globally.
•	Pioneered renewable energy and environmental justice initiatives.
•	Developed innovative technologies for disaster readiness and sustainability.

Our Focus Areas
1.	Education & Training: Delivering evidence-based resources to build disaster-ready communities.
2.	Animal Safety & Welfare: Coordinating nationwide rescues and promoting humane solutions.
3.	Environmental Stewardship: Advancing renewable energy and environmental justice.
4.	Innovation & Technology: Developing scalable solutions for health equity and sustainability.

Our Values
The Eight Pillars of Resilience—Preparedness, Response, Recovery, Education, Equity, Collaboration, Innovation, and Sustainability—guide our work and ensure a holistic approach to global challenges.

Join Us
Whether you're an individual, organization, or policymaker, we invite you to collaborate with us. Together, we can drive impactful change and build a resilient future.

Name: #plantist
Description:
Dr. Gerardo Ceballos (UNAM): "This is no longer a warning about the future — it is a decision about whether you will still have one. #Plantist exists because this is the last window in which human action can still change the outcome."

Melissa Parke (ICAN, Nobel Peace Prize 2017): "Just as the world lives under the constant shadow of nuclear catastrophe, we now stand on the edge of a parallel existential crisis driven by the destruction of Earth's life-support systems. Nuclear weapons could end human life in an instant; ecological collapse could dismantle it irreversibly over time. #Plantist offers a practical pathway to eliminate one of these existential risks by catalyzing a global societal shift toward plant-based eating at the speed this moment demands."

#Plantist is a decentralized, cross-cultural dugnad / ドゥーナド / فزعة / harambee / ujamaa / bayanihan / kasama / gotong royong / adat / mutirão / ñandereko / minga / buen vivir / talkoot / 품앗이 / 結 / 互助 / श्रमदान / ayni / sumak kawsay / ubuntu / isikhathi / nnoboa / meitheal / imece / همیاری / ลงแขก / tương trợ / tequio / fa’a samoa / talanoa / vā / laulima / malama ’āina / kaitiakitanga / nindoodemag / mitakuye oyasin / avatittinnik kamatsiarniq / sila / kanyini / dadirri / अहिंसा परमो धर्मः / सङ्घ / मैत्री / ཕན་ཚུན་རོགས་པ — a high-impact survival intervention guided by independent plant-based catalysts in all 196 nations on Earth, applying social diffusion to end the primary driver of ecological collapse: global meat consumption.

WHO IS A PLANTIST?

A plant-based individual who identifies as a person committed to the survival of humanity and the living world.

WHAT IS PLANTISM?

The recognition that the only way to end and prevent civilizational collapse is to be and stay a plantist.

WHAT IS #PLANTIST?

A global initiative where every person on Earth turns into a plantist in order to end civilizational collapse.

WHO IS A CATALYST AT #PLANTIST?

A plantist who actively grows the number of plantists.

Name: United Kingdom International Search & Rescue Team
Description:
WHO WE ARE

Over the past three decades, UK ISAR has established itself as the premier international search and rescue team, serving as the official UK Government response funded by the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO).

UK ISAR is renowned for its expertise, dedication, and unwavering commitment to saving lives in the most challenging situations, including onset disasters. The team can deploy within a 6-10 hour timeframe from a designated national point of departure, a capability honed over more than 31 years.

UK ISAR includes personnel from 14 of the UK’s Fire & Rescue Services, with support from volunteers in organisations outside of UK Fire & Rescue Services including USAR Medical Teams, Structural Engineers and Vets.

WHAT WE DO

UK ISAR is a team of highly trained personnel that come together for the purpose of International Search and Rescue.

The team responds rapidly to humanitarian emergencies including sudden onset disasters internationally on behalf of the UK Government through the FCDO Emergency Deployable Team 2 (EDT2) programme.

UK ISAR provides a specialised, technical search and rescue response to incidents in disaster affected nations. UK ISAR was one of the first to undertake the United Nations INSARAG classification in 2005 and then went on to be reclassified in 2009 and 2016, and most recent in November 2023.

UK ISAR is currently one of the leading United Nations INSARAG classified search and rescue teams.

Name: 30Forward
Description:
A national movement that is inspired by the U.S. 30×30 initiative, 30Forward is uniquely designed to address the specific challenges and opportunities for women in law enforcement across Canada.

This initiative calls on police leaders to take a pledge with measurable, actionable commitments to recruit, retain, and advance women in policing while fostering an inclusive and supportive workplace culture. Despite progress, women remain underrepresented in policing, particularly in leadership roles. Research shows that increasing the number of women in policing leads to better community engagement, improved public trust, and more effective policing strategies. However, systemic barriers, including recruitment challenges, workplace culture, work-life balance, and a lack of mentorship opportunities, continue to hinder progress.

Our Commitment will have measurable actions in the areas of:

> Recruitment & Hiring
> Retention & Family Support
> Mentorship & Leadership Development
> Allyship & Inclusive Culture
> Building a National Resource Hub
> And More!

Name: Flood Relief GB
Description:
Flood Relief GB – Talidass Aid Mission is a humanitarian campaign dedicated to supporting the flood-affected people of Talidass village and the wider Ghizer district in Gilgit-Baltistan. Following a catastrophic glacier burst in Talidass Nala, the Ghizer River was blocked for hours, reversing its flow and causing massive destruction. Talidass village has been almost completely destroyed, with over 50 homes and properties lost. Across Ghizer, more than 100+ houses have been severely impacted.

This campaign aims to:  
- Raise awareness of the ongoing crisis in Talidass and surrounding areas.  
- Mobilize funds and resources for food, shelter, medical aid, and relocation support.  
- Collaborate with local authorities, NGOs, and community members to ensure transparent and effective aid distribution.  
- Provide long-term support to rebuild the lives and livelihoods of the affected families.
We believe that through unity, compassion, and collective effort, we can restore hope and dignity to the people who have lost everything. Your support—whether through donations, logistics, or sharing this cause—can make a real difference.

Join us in this mission of mercy and resilience.
#FloodReliefGB #TalidassAidMission #HelpGhizer #RebuildTalidass #GilgitBaltistan #DisasterRelief #HumanityFirst

Name: HOJO Solutions
Description:
HOJO Solutions s'est donné comme objectif d'apporter des soins médicaux là où c'est impossible, là où la décision médicale est rendue compliquée et incertaine. 

Pour répondre à cette question difficile: "faut-il évacuer le blessé ou peut-on le soigner sur place ?" HOJO Solutions a développé une application pour aider les non-professionnels de santé dans des sites isolés. Ce peut-être les marins sur les navires dans les eaux internationales, les personnels sur les sites miniers ou les plateformes pétrolières, les secouristes dans les postes avancés des ONG, les intervenants des sociétés d’assistance, les vigiles et agents de sécurité mais aussi les soldats sur des terrains particulièrement difficiles et reculés... Ils peuvent ainsi établir un bilan de secourisme complet pour le transmettre à une autorité médicale distante.

En s'inspirant de la Fiche d'Observation Médicale de Purpan, cette application se présente sous la forme d'une fiche médicale sur tablette, facile à utiliser et enrichie d'aides contextuelles pour non-pro de santé. L'application intègre également de l'Intelligence Artificielle pour faciliter la collecte de données telles que les fréquences cardiaques et respiratoires.

En option, et en fonction de la situation du client, HOJO Solutions propose aussi une mallette durcie, équipée de dispositifs médicaux connectés choisis par le client qui remplissent les champs du formulaire automatiquement (par exemple un électrocardiogramme 12 dérivations mais le client peut ajouter à sa demande  un thermomètre, un tensiomètre…).
Ce bilan complété est aussi transmissible à l’autorité médicale compétente.

Enfin, l'application est basée sur une architecture "par bloc personnalisable" qui permet d'adapter la fiche patient aux spécificités métiers, contexte spécial (tremblement de terre au Maroc le 8 septembre 2023, inondations en Lybie le 17 septembre...) et contraintes de l'opérateur.

In autumn 2021 West Mercia Local Resilience Forum received financial support from the government's LRF Innovation Fund to develop a degree apprenticeship to support the vision for the UK to become a resilient nation.

In May 2023 the Occupational Standard for Resilience and Emergencies Professional was approved and published.

Over 101 employers supported the Trailblazer group in phase 1, through workshops, interviews and meeting discussions, to develop the occupational standard and end point assessment plan – a huge thank you to everyone who contributed.
 
We now move to support training providers to understand what employers need and want from degree-level training provision, and support employers to work together across UK Resilience to access REP apprenticeship providers and ensure the best opportunities for our future apprentices.

Name: Strategic Response Partners
Description:
Strategic Response Partners, Inc. (SRP)  is a unique network of experienced disaster management professionals who serve property owners in nearly all aspects of disaster preparation, response and recovery. We guide each client through catastrophic situations, providing on-site project leadership, consulting before, during and after events like hurricanes, wildfires, tsunamis, tornadoes and earthquakes. With a wealth of military, fire, law enforcement, construction, legal, public adjusting, environmental, business and communications experience, we have been among first responders supporting recovery efforts for all types of major disasters throughout the world for over 29 years.

To date we have facilitated the restoration of 4,000+ properties and have provided expert services on 7,500+ storm & earthquake related projects. Relevant experiences include Hurricanes Andrew, Allison, Erin, Opel, Ivan, Charley, Katrina, Wilma, Gustav, Ike, Irene, Irma, Florence, Michael, and even “Superstorm” Sandy. 

For more information about our catastrophe response, and to find out how we can help you and your customers, please email us at contact@srp24.com or visit our webpage to schedule a consultation.

Name: ISN Maskwa
Description:
Established in 2020 by Missanabie Cree First Nation, ISN Maskwa is Canada’s first Indigenous Emergency Operations Centre (IEOC), providing Indigenous-led community support. 

Headquartered in Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario on the traditional territory of the Anishinaabek, ISN Maskwa’s deployable teams strive to establish and build upon meaningful relationships with First Nations, Metis, and Inuit leaders and communities across the country. 

Our core business pillars provide Indigenous communities with a single point of contact for Evacuations and Emergency Response, Investigations, Training, Security, and Community Safety and Well-Being Planning.

Name: IRG Global Emergency Management
Description:
Innovative Response Group Global Emergency Management is dedicated to providing exceptional emergency management and crisis response services. Our mission is to be an indispensable partner to governments and communities, offering comprehensive logistics, expert guidance, and unwavering professionalism across all phases of emergency management.

With a commitment to education, precision, and cutting-edge technology, we lead the way in delivering innovative turnkey solutions that enhance preparedness, response, and recovery efforts. From disaster planning to on-the-ground crisis coordination, we work tirelessly to build resilience, ensure public safety, and support communities when they need it most.

Based in Florida and serving beyond, IRG Global is redefining emergency management through leadership, compassion, and expertise—because in times of disaster, every second counts.

Name: TCS Ambulance
Description:
TCS Ambulance zählt rund 400 spezialisierte Mitarbeitende, die sich voll und ganz der Rettung und dem Krankentransport widmen. Sie ist in den 9 Kantonen Aargau, Basel, Bern, Freiburg, Luzern, Zug, Zürich sowie Genf und Waadt vertreten. Insgesamt besteht die Flotte aus etwa 57 Fahrzeugen und 22 Stützpunkten. Damit werden über 45'000 Einsätze pro Jahr bewältigt. Dank föderalistischer Struktur und der lokalen Verankerung sind wir in der besonderen und exklusiven Lage, in der ganzen Schweiz einheitliche und qualitativ hochstehende Leistungen zu erbringen.

TCS Ambulance compte plus de 400 collaboratrices et collaborateurs spécialisés entièrement dédiés au secours d’urgence et au transport de patients, avec une présence dans 9 cantons : Fribourg, Genève, Vaud ainsi que Bâle, Berne, Zoug, Argovie, Lucerne et Zurich. Ils peuvent compter sur une flotte d'environ 57 véhicules et 22 bases logistiques pour accomplir plus de 45'000 interventions par année. Grâce à notre structure fédéraliste et notre ancrage local, nous sommes en mesure d’offrir des prestations homogènes et de haute qualité dans toute la Suisse.

TCS Ambulance conta circa 400 dipendenti specializzati che si dedicano completamente al soccorso e al trasporto di pazienti. È presente in 9 cantoni: Argovia, Basilea, Berna, Friborgo, Lucerna, Zugo, Zurigo, Ginevra e Vaud. In totale, la flotta è composta da circa 57 veicoli e 22 basi logistiche. Ciò significa che ogni anno vengono effettuate oltre 45'000 missioni. Grazie alla nostra struttura federalista e al nostro radicamento locale, siamo nella posizione unica ed esclusiva di poter fornire servizi standardizzati e di alta qualità in tutta la Svizzera.
